ObjectStates.Name: Custom_Assetbundle
ObjectStates.LuaScript:
ButtonColors = {White = {1, 1, 1}, Brown = {0.443, 0.231, 0.09}, Red = {0.856, 0.1, 0.094}, Orange = {0.956, 0.392, 0.113},
    Yellow = {0.905, 0.898, 0.172}, Green = {0.192, 0.701, 0.168}, Teal = {0.129, 0.694, 0.607}, Blue = {0.118, 0.53, 1},
    Purple = {0.627, 0.125, 0.941}, Pink = {0.96, 0.439, 0.807}}

SixSymbolColor = {White = '[FFFFFF]', Brown = '[703A16]', Red = '[DA1917]', Orange = '[F3631C]',
    Yellow = '[E6E42B]', Green = '[30B22A]', Teal = '[20B09A]', Blue = '[1E87FF]',
    Purple = '[9F1FEF]', Pink = '[F46FCD]'}

ColorKor = {White = '흰색', Brown = '갈색', Red = '빨간색', Orange = '주황색',
    Yellow = '노란색', Green = '녹색', Teal = '청록색', Blue = '파란색',
    Purple = '보라색', Pink = '핑크색'}

--Runs when the scripted button inside the button is clicked
function buttonPress()
    if lockout == false then
        --Call on any other function here. For example:
        --Global.call("Function Name", {table of parameters if needed})
        --You could also add your own scrting here. For example:
        --print("The button was pressed. Hoozah.")
        FirstPlayer()
        self.AssetBundle.playTriggerEffect(0) --triggers animation/sound
        lockout = true --locks out the button
        startLockoutTimer() --Starts up a timer to remove lockout
    end
end


function FirstPlayer()
    q = 1
    PlayerColors = {}
    PlayerNames = {}
    for _, p in ipairs(getSeatedPlayers()) do
    PlayerColors[q] = Player[p].color
    PlayerNames[q] = Player[p].steam_name
    q = q+1
    end

    RandomPlayer = math.random(1, #PlayerColors) + os.date('%S')
    RandomPlayer = math.fmod(RandomPlayer,#PlayerColors)
    if RandomPlayer < 0 then RandomPlayer = RandomPlayer + #PlayerColors end
    RandomPlayer = RandomPlayer + 1

    for i=1, #PlayerColors, 1 do
        if RandomPlayer == i then self.setColorTint(ButtonColors[PlayerColors[i]]) end
    end

    AllPlayersString = ''
    for i=1, #PlayerColors, 1 do
        if i != q-1 then
        AllPlayersString = AllPlayersString .. tostring(ColorKor[PlayerColors[i]]) .. ', '
        else
        AllPlayersString = AllPlayersString .. tostring(ColorKor[PlayerColors[i]])
        end
    end

    pl = PlayerColors[RandomPlayer]
    broadcastToAll('[FFFFFF](' .. AllPlayersString .. ')[7F7F7F] 플레이어들 중 ' .. SixSymbolColor[tostring(pl)]..tostring(ColorKor[pl])..tostring(' '..PlayerNames[RandomPlayer]).. '[7F7F7F] 님이 [i]선 플레이어 입니다[/i]',  {0.5, 0.5, 0.5})
end

--Runs on load, creates button and makes sure the lockout is off
function onload()
    self.createButton({
        label="", click_function = "buttonPress",
        function_owner = self,  position={0,0.25,0}, height=1400, width=1400
    })
    self.createButton({
        label="누가\n선이야?", click_function = "buttonPress",
        function_owner = self,  position={0,0.65,0}, height=0, width=0, color = {1,0,0}, font_color={0,0,0}, font_size=400
    })
    lockout = false
end

--Starts a timer that, when it ends, will unlock the button
function startLockoutTimer()
    Timer.create({identifier=self.getGUID(), function_name='unlockLockout', delay=0.5})
end

--Unlocks button
function unlockLockout()
    lockout = false
end

--Ends the timer if the object is destroyed before the timer ends, to prevent an error
function onDestroy()
    Timer.destroy(self.getGUID())
end
